examples.title: Array Declaration and Initialization
examples.description: Demonstrates various ways to declare and initialize one-dimensional arrays in C
examples.explanation: Shows 5 different methods of array initialization in C. Partial initialization fills remaining elements with 0. Omitting size lets compiler deduce it from initializer list.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for declaration, O(n) for printing
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is array size

examples.title: Finding Largest and Smallest Element
examples.description: Program to find the maximum and minimum values in an integer array using single pass
examples.explanation: Uses single traversal algorithm. Start with first element as both max and min. Compare each subsequent element and update accordingly. This is more efficient than comparing with both max and min separately in each iteration.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - single pass through n elements
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - only using max, min and loop variable

examples.title: Two-Dimensional Array Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates declaration, initialization, and traversal of a 2D array in row-major order
examples.explanation: Shows nested loop traversal of 2D array. Outer loop iterates through rows, inner loop through columns. In C, 2D arrays are stored in row-major order—elements of row 0 come first, then row 1, etc.
examples.timeComplexity: O(rows × cols) = O(m×n) for traversal and sum
examples.spaceComplexity: O(rows × cols) for storing the array, O(1) for sum variable

examples.title: Pointer Arithmetic with Arrays
examples.description: Demonstrates relationship between array name and pointer, and pointer arithmetic
examples.explanation: Demonstrates that array name acts as constant pointer to first element. *(arr+i) is equivalent to arr[i]. When pointer increments (ptr+1), it moves by sizeof(element_type) bytes. This shows how arrays and pointers are closely related in C.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the array
